title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After an initial meeting where Dr. Kennedy vaguely confirms that a sanatorium involved was a tuberculosis facility, Jiles and Gwendolyn depart, feeling that the doctor is withholding information. Despite Jiles's hesitation to continue the investigation, Gwendolyn insists on pursuing the truth regarding her father, Calvin Harley.

The following afternoon, Dr. Kennedy calls Jiles to arrange a visit. While waiting, Gwendolyn speaks with Old Foster, who describes Dr. Kennedy as a good but dull doctor who stopped practicing fifteen years ago due to ill health. Foster also mentions that Dr. Kennedy's sister was considered "wild" and ran off with another man.

When Dr. Kennedy arrives at three o'clock, he reveals that the facility Calvin Harley entered was not a tuberculosis sanatorium, but a mental institution. He explains that Calvin suffered a mental breakdown due to extreme stress after his wife, Helen, left him for another man. Dr. Kennedy asserts that Calvin’s belief that he had strangled Helen was merely a "bewitching delusion" caused by pathological fantasies and unsettling dreams. 

Dr. Kennedy recounts a specific incident where Calvin arrived at his examination room and claimed, "I killed Helen." Gwendolyn notes that when they inspected the bedroom, there was no body and no sign of a struggle. Dr. Kennedy explains that he found a note from Helen in a living room wastebasket, in which she stated she was leaving him for the man she loved. Dr. Kennedy concludes that Calvin saw the note and, in a state of mental instability, hallucinated the murder.
